scaled_oracle_cases.description: Scaled version of seed case covering extensionless file detection, YAML comment skipping, duplicate compounds, and randomized filenames to satisfy D, C, and A operators.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the solution handles extensionless files (detected by content), skips comment lines in YAML, yields duplicate compound entries, and accepts arbitrary file paths without hardcoding.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Solution must correctly parse extensionless TSV files, ignore YAML comment lines, include all entries including duplicates, and work with any filename (not hardcoded).
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Solution fails to parse extensionless files (raises ParseError), includes comment lines as entries, or fails to yield duplicate entries, or assumes fixed filenames.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
import uuid, os
prefix = 'scaled_' + uuid.uuid4().hex[:8] + '_'
tsv_path = prefix + 'exchange.tsv'
with open(tsv_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('Glucose\t-10\t100\n')
    f.write('Lactate\t0\t50\n')
    f.write('Glucose\t-5\t80\n')
yaml_path = prefix + 'exchange.yaml'
with open(yaml_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('# comment line\n')
    f.write('Glucose:-10:100:cytosol\n')
    f.write('O2:-1000:1000:mitochondria\n')
ext_path = prefix + 'exchange'
with open(ext_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('Xylose\t-1\t10\n')
print('Files created.')
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new input/data complexity introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new anti-shortcut robustness requirement introduced by this operator.
